target: data/wins.json WIN-010 detail_verdict_text + detail_evidence — c9 defense-neutralization (ISS-1496, advocate defense survives 3/5) bundled with three persisting minor holes (deferred c6/c7/c8/c9). Four targeted substring replacements: H1 (moderate, c9) rewrites the ≤30% Kim & Chang parenthetical in detail_verdict_text to a citation-honest conservative-upper-bound framing that acknowledges Kim & Chang (2018) does not tabulate a specific attenuation factor for >50° CGM stations, preempting the 'number pulled from a paper that does not contain it' defender attack per curmudgeon option (b). H2 (minor, persisting c6→c9) softens 'drift both residuals in parallel' to 'approximately common multiplicative error at first order'. H3 (minor, persisting c6→c9) updates the precision claim in detail_evidence from '0.1% at seven stations, 1.8% at the worst' to '0.6% at seven of nine stations, 2.78% at the worst' (matching the already-patched detail_verdict_text in today's decider run). H4 (minor, persisting c6→c9) softens 'any smooth monotonic H(r)' to 'a broad family of smooth monotonic H(r) functions fitted to the same sun/moon altitude calibration targets'. No edits to claim, finding, verdict, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, detail_extra, code_analysis, or detail_group. Operative verdict 'Std Model Explains' unchanged.
assigned_exp_collision_note: ISS-1496.assigned_exp = 'EXP-240' but EXP-240 is already in use on disk (EXP-240-WIN069-circuity-citation-retraction-iss1487-iss1488.json, Apr 23). Tracker.next_id was stale at 238 while disk held EXP-238 through EXP-242. Allocating EXP-243 for this work to avoid overwrite; decider should reconcile the assigned_exp field on ISS-1496 to EXP-243 (or ignore the field and use findings_file from the issue-proposal).
patch_instructions: Apply all four substring replacements atomically under ISS-1496. Each has been verified to have exactly 1 occurrence in its target field. After applying, re-run the WIN-010 c10 curmudgeon review; expect the advocate_mode.defense_survives to drop from 3/5 to ≤2/5 because the parenthetical now explicitly discloses the absence of a paper-tabulated attenuation factor (defender can no longer charge 'fabricated from a paper that does not contain it' — we now say that ourselves). All three minor persisting holes close simultaneously.

patches.rationale: Curmudgeon c9 option (b) — reframe the ≤30% bound as a conservative upper bound in the absence of paper-tabulated attenuation. Kim & Chang (2018) documents systematic decrease outside 30°–50° but does not give a specific attenuation factor for >50° CGM stations. By stating this explicitly, we preempt the 'you made the number up' defender attack and convert the pre-registration from 'appeal to paper authority' to 'conservative first-order bound with acknowledged uncertainty'. Honest uncertainty is stronger than false precision. The ≤30% remains falsifiable: if the 2026-08-12 LER residual comes in at >30% of the in-band median, our pre-registered prediction FAILS, and we commit in print to replacing the bound with a paper-derived amplitude once the 39-eclipse binned data are accessible. This is a stronger epistemic position than the original parenthetical.
patches.before: suppressed to ≤30% of the ensemble-median in-band signal (consistent with Kim & Chang (2018)'s published attenuation envelope for latitudes outside the primary response zone)
patches.after: suppressed to ≤30% of the ensemble-median in-band signal — a conservative upper bound adopted in the absence of a paper-tabulated attenuation factor for >50° CGM stations. Kim & Chang (2018) documents a systematic Z-component decrease outside the 30°–50° CGM band across their 39-eclipse ensemble but does not itself tabulate the magnitude of the drop-off for the >50° stations — so we fix the upper bound at 30% of the in-band median as a first-order, citation-honest pre-registration, with the explicit acknowledgement that if the 39-eclipse binned amplitudes become accessible we will replace this bound with a paper-derived amplitude and re-pre-register.

patches.rationale: Persisting c6→c9 minor: 'drift both residuals in parallel, leaving the fingerprint intact' is too strong for a pre-registration document because E-region ionospheric conductivity has magnetic-latitude structure (dip-equator enhancement, auroral-zone enhancement) that can make nonlinear-coverage corrections mag-lat-dependent. The softened phrasing preserves the discriminator argument but admits that the corrections are approximately-common (first-order) rather than strictly-parallel.
patches.before: any nonlinear-coverage effect will drift both residuals in parallel, leaving the geographic-vs-magnetic-latitude fingerprint intact
patches.after: any nonlinear-coverage effect will introduce an approximately common multiplicative error on both residuals at first order, so the geographic-vs-magnetic-latitude fingerprint remains the dominant discriminator even under moderate nonlinear-coverage corrections

patches.rationale: Persisting c6→c9 minor: the '0.1% at seven stations, 1.8% at the worst' FSF-precision claim is overstated by ~5× on the 'seven of nine' figure (actual: 1/9 at <0.1%, 7/9 at <0.5%, 8/9 at <1.0%, worst ~2.16% at SPT). Today's earlier decider run already patched detail_verdict_text to the truer '0.6% at seven of nine stations, 2.78% at the worst'; this patch propagates the same update to detail_evidence so the two fields agree. Pearson r = 0.9999 is correct to 4 decimal places and stays. 'seven of nine' is slight overstatement (actual ~7/9 at <0.5% — matches the 0.6% threshold to within rounding); keeping 'seven of nine' preserves consistency with the already-patched detail_verdict_text wording.
patches.before: reproduces all nine published FSF values (0.642–2.075) to better than 0.1% at seven stations and within 1.8% at the worst, with Pearson r = 0.9999
patches.after: reproduces all nine published FSF values (0.642–2.075) to within 0.6% at seven of nine stations and within 2.78% at the worst, with Pearson r = 0.9999

patches.rationale: Persisting c6→c9 minor: 'any smooth monotonic H(r) ... produces a latitude-dependent ratio of the same form' is an unsupported universal. The softened phrasing ('a broad family of smooth monotonic H(r) functions fitted to the same sun/moon altitude calibration targets ... produces a latitude-dependent ratio of effectively the same form over the relevant r range') retains the discriminator argument (alternative disc-geometry models with comparable height functions are not distinguished by FSF fit quality) while scoping the claim to the family of calibration-compatible H(r) functions, which is defensible without needing to prove the universal.
patches.before: any smooth monotonic H(r) combined with the dome's r·tan(θ) = H(r) convention produces a latitude-dependent ratio of the same form
patches.after: a broad family of smooth monotonic H(r) functions fitted to the same sun/moon altitude calibration targets, combined with the dome's r·tan(θ) = H(r) convention, produces a latitude-dependent ratio of effectively the same form over the relevant r range

holes_addressed.resolved: H3 patch (propagation to detail_evidence; dvt already patched today)

new_evidence_added: Explicit statement that Kim & Chang (2018) does not tabulate a specific attenuation factor for >50° CGM stations — this is new, citation-honest meta-evidence that preempts a concrete defender attack.

anticipated_objections.objection: Isn't admitting Kim & Chang doesn't tabulate the number weaker than citing it as if they did?
anticipated_objections.response: Only if the number is actually in the paper. The curmudgeon's c9 quantitative_verification confirms the paper does not itself tabulate a >50° CGM attenuation factor (it does document systematic decrease, but not the magnitude). A citation that claims support the paper does not provide is a fabrication, and a defender reading the paper will catch it and destroy our credibility. Honest uncertainty beats false precision — the ≤30% remains a falsifiable upper bound, and if the 2026-08-12 LER residual comes in at >30%, the pre-registration fails regardless of citation framing. By stating the citation gap ourselves, we convert a potential fatal defender attack ('you made the number up') into an acknowledged limitation that strengthens the pre-registration's epistemic standing.

anticipated_objections.objection: Shouldn't the ≤30% be replaced by a figure-derived number instead of softened framing?
anticipated_objections.response: If the 39-eclipse binned amplitudes become accessible we will do exactly that — the new prose commits in print to replacing the bound with a paper-derived amplitude once the data allow it (this commitment is itself falsifiable — either the bound gets tightened or we have to acknowledge we never found the data). In the interim, ≤30% as a conservative first-order upper bound is the cleanest move: it's specific enough to fail on a plausible 40%+ residual at LER, and it doesn't require a citation that doesn't exist.

anticipated_objections.objection: Won't a within-cluster-variance reframe (curmudgeon option c) be stronger?
anticipated_objections.response: Possibly, but it would require substantial re-architecture of the 2026 test design and reverse the rhetorical thrust of the binary-classifier disclosure (ISS-1485) that was only just added. Option (b) — the citation-honest framing — is strictly safer for this cycle: it closes the c9 moderate hole without disturbing the already-accepted binary-classifier disclosure. If the cross-WIN propagation work (WIN-025, WIN-068) later chooses the within-cluster-variance design, WIN-010 can follow then; for now, option (b) matches the curmudgeon's stated 'fastest and cheapest' recommendation.

kernel_of_truth: The advocate's defense survives 3/5 because they correctly identified a real weakness: the ≤30% bound was asserted as 'consistent with Kim & Chang (2018)'s published attenuation envelope' in a way that implied paper-tabulated support for the specific factor. The advocate's charge would be devastating if unrebutted: a number pulled from a paper that doesn't contain it. This patch acknowledges the kernel of truth (the paper doesn't tabulate) and converts it from a weakness into a strength by turning implicit false precision into explicit honest uncertainty.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_the_dome: Acknowledging the citation gap does not rescue the dome's WIN-010 claim because the operative verdict ('Std Model Explains') turns on the 2017 BOU −10.9 nT anchoring and the Chapman/Sq mechanism, not on the ≤30% 2026 pre-registration. The ≤30% is our offered pre-registered test for a FUTURE eclipse (2026-08-12); it is not the reason the 2017 claim is already-explained. The dome cannot convert a better-framed out-of-band bound in our pre-registration into support for the 2017 anchoring problem, the Kp asymmetry, the post-hoc finding, or the refraction contamination. The three minor persisting holes (H2/H3/H4) all operate at the same tier: they sharpen the future-test prose without re-opening the 2017 non-discrimination argument.
cross_win_consistency_flag: WIN-025 (2024 Chile) and WIN-068 (2024 N.Am.) carry earlier, less-specific Chapman/Kim & Chang refutation language without the 30°–50° CGM-band classifier, ≤30% quantification, or the binary-classifier disclosure. Post-patch, WIN-010 continues to carry the most-developed pre-registration language. Future cross-WIN propagation (if pursued) must decide whether to (a) propagate the citation-honest ≤30%-as-upper-bound framing to WIN-025/068, or (b) scope the quantitative bound to WIN-010's 2026-08-12 eclipse only (defensible because WIN-025/068 describe PAST eclipses — the ≤30% is a prospective test). Option (b) is recommended: the ≤30% is a pre-registration for a specific future event, not a retrospective statement about past eclipses.
out_of_scope_deliberately: Within-cluster-variance reframe (curmudgeon option c) — deferred, out of scope per curmudgeon's own 'fastest and cheapest' recommendation.
Cross-WIN propagation to WIN-025/WIN-068 — a separate cleanup EXP per EXP-234's own cross_win_consistency_flag.

Locating a specific Kim & Chang (2018) figure/table that supports a quantitative attenuation factor for >50° CGM stations — if such a figure exists, a future EXP can upgrade option (b) to option (a) with a tighter, paper-derived bound. For now, the curmudgeon verified that the paper does not tabulate it.
propagation_note: No TLDR edits. The tldr_evidence ('Eclipses cause small magnetic dips because the moon's shadow reduces ionospheric conductivity — this has been known since Chapman described it in 1933. The dome's observed -10.9 nT at Boulder is exactly what this standard mechanism predicts, to within measurement uncertainty.') and tldr_verdict ('The Chapman ionospheric mechanism, documented since 1933 and quantitatively confirmed by Kim & Chang (2018) across 39 eclipses, predicts this signal without dome parameters. The dome does genuinely derive a station-to-station scaling (FSF = H(r)/r) but it anchors that scaling to the 2017 Boulder observation itself, reusing the same −10.9 nT value as both confirmed WIN and 2026 baseline — a ratio between stations is not a prediction of the signal, and the absolute magnitude is still borrowed from the data.') both remain factually correct after patch and do not need updating — they summarize the 2017 non-discrimination argument, which this patch does not touch. See c9 tldr_review.notes.
next_cycle_expectation: c10 curmudgeon review should find advocate_mode.defense_survives ≤ 2/5 (the citation-gap defender attack is self-rebutted). The three persisting minor holes (H2/H3/H4) drop from 'persisting c6→c9' to 'resolved at c9→c10'. Operative verdict 'Std Model Explains' remains unchanged. If advocate_mode finds a new line of attack on the reframed prose (e.g., 'your upper bound is too lax', 'your commitment to replace the bound is unenforceable'), that is a new hole, not a persistence of H1.
